title: Living Graph Math Codex Creation
author_source_note: Imported from a local standalone theory draft surfaced during project discussion.
summary_short: Long-horizon query math and topological field doctrine for later BD graph/query work.
summary_verbose: This codex captures a future-state mathematical doctrine for treating the living graph as a richer topological and field-shaped substrate. It is useful as a north-star for later query-time lenses, contradiction-aware traversal, higher-order topology, and manifold-style memory ideas. It is not a truthful description of the current Bootstrap Nucleus or the current Phase 1 Emitter bottleneck solution.
allowed_claims: The project carries a five-surface neuron doctrine.
Later query-time work may benefit from richer topological and field-style math.
The codex is useful as a north-star reference for future query/lens thinking.
disallowed_claims: The current Bootstrap Nucleus already implements contradiction-as-curvature.
The current Emitter already uses physics-lens execution.
The current cross-document bottleneck is solved by the codex math.
The codex is active runtime architecture truth.
why_valuable: It gives language for later query-time topology texture and field behavior.
It can guide future contradiction, lens, and manifold doctrine without contaminating current runtime truth.
It helps separate long-horizon math from immediate ingest bottleneck work.
